plugins.name: freelunch
plugins.description: Speed-first plugin that forces every task onto concurrent background Sonnet agents with zero idle time

plugins.name: terse
plugins.description: Output-token compression scoped to conversational prose — never worker prompts, contracts, code, or safety-critical text. Three levels via /terse.

plugins.name: blueprint
plugins.description: Archetype-based architecture selection from a queryable database: deterministic classify/recommend CLI, anti-pattern checks, and the fan-out contract to freeze before dispatching workers.

plugins.name: no-mock
plugins.description: Steers deliverables toward production-runnable structure at generation time — real persistence, real integration seams, no silent mocks. Pure direction, no verification machinery.

plugins.name: scout
plugins.description: Pre-build reconnaissance: identify best-in-class exemplars and the customer expectation baseline (Camp benchmarking + Kano + saturation stop rule), compress into a scout brief that steers the build. Bounded and judgment-gated — never a research report, never post-build comparison.

plugins.name: no-footgun
plugins.description: Direction-only security steering: names the threat patterns for the surface being built so the secure pattern is chosen at write time — surface-gated, cascading custom rules, zero review passes or scans.

plugins.name: doctrine
plugins.description: Documentation placement doctrine: six lifetime-based buckets under docs/, created on session start, a directive that classifies at write time and records decisions/measurements as they happen, and a PreToolUse gate refusing writes under docs/ outside them.

plugins.name: warrant
plugins.description: Work-unit protocol: one approval gate at the front, then uninterrupted execution. A proposal states the request, the constraints, and the write set; approval freezes it; a PreToolUse gate refuses edits outside it and commits without the Proposal: trailer; SessionStart rebuilds state from the repository.

plugins.name: dispatch
plugins.description: Git-as-sole-channel protocol: an agent communicates with the oracle above it only through git, so every report is a git write and every git event is a trigger — report IS the next actor's trigger. Reports route by lifetime; a needed decision is delivered to the remote as a blocking marker and label, not asked in chat, and the run terminates rather than idling. A PreToolUse gate holds the oracle boundary; SessionStart rebuilds the parked state from the remote.

plugins.name: tokenmaxxxer-env
plugins.description: One-install environment bundle: installs the whole tokenmaxxxer steering stack via dependencies.
